key_points: Freshmen cannot choose their dorm; BSU assigns based on major
Stu West has persistent weed smell and sanitation horror stories
University apartments have 1960s appliances and no central AC
key_quotes: Just be ready for 24/7 weed smell
They found someone shit in the showers last semester. Good luck diva
The university apartments are fine. They just lack central AC and have appliances from the 1960s

key_points: Frat parties are weak; house parties occasionally get shot up
Getting out of your dorm room is critical to making friends
Strong campus event culture with free stuff and broad club options
key_quotes: If you like parties our frat parties ain't allat great anymore, and the house parties get shot up a bit more often than I'm comfortable with
My biggest regret freshman year was staying in my dorm so often. Go to all the events!
Go to all the events! Even if some seem corny lol, you can often get cool free stuff

key_points: Meal swipe value has declined significantly year over year
Dining Plus credit barely lasts mid-semester now; it used to last all semester
Squirrel spotted in Taco Bell; multiple reports of food poisoning on campus
key_quotes: I remember when i started here the fall of 2022 i could make my dining plus last all semester plus some, but now it barely gets me through to the middle of the semester
With the amount of times I got food poisoning at ball state I am honestly not surprised
A meal swipe is $9.65 for Lunch and Dinner, and $5.85 for breakfast

key_points: Self-inflicted gunshot wound incident reported in Stu West dorm
Seasonal illness wave hits every fall; students call it the 'Muncie Plague'
No direct data on counseling services; support resources not mentioned by students
key_quotes: Self inflicted gunshot wound on the 6th floor. To the face— victim was alive and was able to walk themself to ambulance
Its the Muncie Plague that happens every fall semester
Ball State always brings on way too many students but it works out because of how many drop out around this time

key_points: First Day textbook program routinely overcharges students significantly
Presidential scholarship maxes out at $5,000/year; students feel this is insufficient
International scholarship of $14k/year exists but tied to GPA minimums
key_quotes: For me, they wanted to charge about $477, which is a little over double what I would have to pay if I buy them on my own
This program is so stupid. Why am I automatically charged for 15 credit hours when I don't even require any materials
The presidential is only 5,000 a year. I fear that is not enough

key_points: Journalism and CCIM programs are genuinely strong with David Letterman ties
Media majors face major class availability issues; some locked out of needed courses
Social work program praised for hands-on community placement
key_quotes: Ball State has one of the best J-Schools in the midwest, and CCIM also has a lot of great programs
I'm a media major and theres literally no classes I can take that I need to meet my degree requirements
I got my bachelors of social work from ball state and one of the things I really liked about the program were the teachers and the hands on aspect

key_points: President Mearns is actively unpopular; booed publicly at community events
ACLU sued BSU twice for First Amendment violations against students and faculty
Professor fired over a Charlie Kirk Facebook post; campus speech climate is chilled
key_quotes: Mearns was one of the judges. He got booed harder than the mayor
id share my thoughts but it seems like doing so may be enough for expulsion. disagree with mearns and youre out
This is an escalation in insanity from the staff firing over her nuanced Charlie Kirk on her own Facebook page

key_points: Campus is walkable and appropriately sized; not overwhelming like Purdue or IU
Muncie is a small city with limited nightlife but cheap off-campus housing
Parking enforcement is aggressive and expensive; students park blocks away to avoid it
key_quotes: Campus is almost the perfect size. Walkable year round
The way this school enforces parking you would swear it makes up for like 50% of the total income for the institution
Most apartments around muncie are 800 or less for a 2 bedroom

key_points: Journalism and CCIM alumni report solid regional connections and industry entry
Accounting program has placed graduates at notable firms including Notre Dame
One alum warns journalism is a 'dying profession' and a poor investment
key_quotes: He worked for a local accounting firm in Keystone for a bit and now works for Notre Dame
Ball State is a great school. It gets unfairly punked on by kids who go to the bigger universities like Purdue and IU
Journalism is the worst college investment you can make. Journalism is a dying profession

key_points: First Day textbook program can double your actual textbook costs; opt out recommended
Meal plan value has eroded; students getting less for same or more money each year
Off-campus housing is cheap but BSU-affiliated rentals get poor reviews
key_quotes: saved my ass for sure, wasn't gonna pay $341.25 for a digital access code and book i can get for $85
i remember when i started here the fall of 2022 i could make my dining plus last all semester plus some
stay away from bsu rentals and middletown

key_points: Friendly, mid-sized campus with approachable professors and tight classroom sizes
Free speech controversies and admin overreach cast a shadow over campus culture
Strong sense of community pride despite Muncie's limitations
key_quotes: It's a very chill campus and people are generally both cool and friendly
ball state arrests and punishes students who use their first amendment rights to free speech and is getting sued by the ACLU
I transferred to IU after my freshman year and I regret it to this day

key_points: **Free Speech**: ACLU filed two lawsuits; professor fired for a Facebook post about Charlie Kirk
**Dining Safety**: Squirrel in dining hall, multiple food poisoning reports, roach sightings reported
**Data Security**: Canvas hacked and held for ransom; BSU delayed notifying students
key_quotes: id share my thoughts but it seems like doing so may be enough for expulsion. disagree with mearns and youre out
With the amount of times I got food poisoning at ball state I am honestly not surprised
Canvas has been hacked and is being held for ransom... Ball State hasn't said a word about it

key_points: David Letterman actively visits campus and hosts celebrity talks for CCIM students
Small classroom environments mean real professor interaction, not lecture-hall anonymity
Opt out of First Day program to save hundreds of dollars on textbooks
key_quotes: communications school is always having david letterman pop in!!!! i think you'd be getting a really good education
Another thing I really enjoy is the small classroom environments. Professors interact and actually know your name
Don't have her buy her textbooks until professors tell her what she needs. Sometimes the 'required textbooks' aren't really required
